messages.content: Edit the Text. All references in the text should be in their place. "Figures 5, 6, and 7 illustrate the reliability diagrams for local and semi-local probabilistic forecasts of 24-hour accumulated precipitation over lead times of 24 hours, 48 hours, and 72 hours. The forecasts are estimated using the democratic voting (raw ensemble forecast), BMA, EMOS-CSG, and EMOS-GEV methods . Various thresholds of 0.1, 2.5, 5, 10, 15, and 25 mm are utilized for the assessment."

Figures 5, 6, and 7 depict the reliability diagrams for local and semi-local probabilistic forecasts of 24-hour accumulated precipitation over lead times of 24, 48, and 72 hours. The forecasts are estimated using the democratic voting (raw ensemble forecast), Bayesian Model Averaging (BMA), Ensemble Model Output Statistics with climatology, persistence, and analogue predictor selection (EMOS-CSG), and Ensemble Model Output Statistics with Generalized Extreme Value distribution fitting (EMOS-GEV) methods. The assessment employs various thresholds of 0.1, 2.5, 5, 10, 15, and 25 mm.

Edit the Text. All references in the text should be in their place. “Figures 5, 6, and 7 depict the reliability diagrams for local and semi-local probabilistic forecasts of 24-hour accumulated precipitation over lead times of 24, 48, and 72 hours. The forecasts are estimated using the democratic voting (raw ensemble forecast),  BMA, EMOS-CSG, and EMOS-GEV methods. The assessment employs various thresholds of 0.1, 2.5, 5, 10, 15, and 25 mm."

The reliability diagrams for local and semi-local probabilistic forecasts of 24-hour accumulated precipitation over lead times of 24, 48, and 72 hours are shown in Figures 5, 6, and 7. The raw ensemble forecast, Bayesian Model Averaging (BMA), Ensemble Model Output Statistics with climatology, persistence, and analogue predictor selection (EMOS-CSG), and Ensemble Model Output Statistics with Generalized Extreme Value distribution fitting (EMOS-GEV) methods are utilized for estimating the forecasts. The assessment includes various thresholds of 0.1, 2.5, 5, 10, 15, and 25 mm.

Edit the Text. All references in the text should be in their place. "As shown in the figures, the predictive PDFs estimated by the raw ensemble are not well calibrated and are close to the “no resolution” line, especially in high thresholds.  Also for the EMOS-GEV method for threshold above 5 not well calibrated for local and semi-local."

The reliability diagrams in Figures 5, 6, and 7 indicate that the predictive probability density functions (PDFs) estimated by the raw ensemble method are not accurately calibrated, as they tend to align with the "no resolution" line, particularly for higher thresholds. Furthermore, the EMOS-GEV method shows poor calibration for local and semi-local when the threshold exceeds 5.

Edit the Text. All references in the text should be in their place. "for the 24-h, 48-h, and 72-h forecasts, two methods of local and semi-local have similar results for all three methods on the thresholds of 0.1 and 2.5 mm."
For the 24-hour, 48-hour, and 72-hour forecasts, both the local and semi-local methods yield comparable results across all three methods for the thresholds of 0.1 and 2.5 mm.

Edit the Text. All references in the text should be in their place. “For the 24-hour, 48-hour, and 72-hour forecasts, both the local and semi-local methods yield comparable results across all three methods for the thresholds of 0.1 and 2.5 mm. for the 24-h, 48-h, and 72-h forecasts, two methods of local and semi-local have similar results for all three methods on the thresholds of 0.1 and 2.5 mm. but on the thresholds of 5,10, 15 and 20 mm semi-local's method  better than than local's; In other words, semi-local's is more reliable."

The local and semi-local methods demonstrate similar outcomes for the 24-hour, 48-hour, and 72-hour forecasts, specifically for the threshold values of 0.1 and 2.5 mm. However, for the threshold values of 5, 10, 15, and 20 mm, the semi-local method outperforms the local method and is deemed more reliable.

Edit the Text. All references in the text should be in their place. “In BMA method, local has better answers. But in EMOS-CSG and EMOS-GEV methods, semi-local has a better answer, or in other words, it is more reliable."

In the Bayesian Model Averaging (BMA) method, the local approach provides superior outcomes. Conversely, for the Ensemble Model Output Statistics with climatology, persistence, and analogue predictor selection (EMOS-CSG) and Ensemble Model Output Statistics with Generalized Extreme Value distribution fitting (EMOS-GEV) methods, the semi-local method yields better results and is therefore deemed more reliable.

Edit the Text. All references in the text should be in their place. "In other words, the reliability of light and medium rainfalls, for the 24-h accumulative rainfall is considerable. In the thresholds of 15 and 25 mm, the reliability curve of the BMA method is better calibrated than that of the EMOS-CSG method. The BMA and EMOS-CSG methods have higher resolution than the raw output of the ensemble system and the EMOS-GEV method in all the lead times. Generally, the BMA method used for the 24-h, 48-h, and 72-h forecasts provides more reliable probabilistic forecasts than the EMOS-CSG and the EMOS-GEV methods and the raw ensemble forecast over studied domain."

To summarize, the reliability of light and moderate precipitation levels for 24-hour accumulated rainfall is noteworthy. Moreover, at threshold values of 15 and 25 mm, the BMA method exhibits better calibration than the EMOS-CSG method. Both the BMA and EMOS-CSG methods offer higher resolutions than the raw ensemble output and the EMOS-GEV method across all lead times. Overall, the BMA method proves to be the most reliable method for 24-hour, 48-hour, and 72-hour forecasts, as compared to the EMOS-CSG and EMOS-GEV methods and the raw ensemble forecast, when evaluated over the studied region.

Edit the Text. All references in the text should be in their place. "To summarize, the reliability of light and moderate precipitation levels for 24-hour accumulated rainfall is noteworthy. Moreover, at threshold values of 15 and 25 mm, the BMA method exhibits better calibration than the EMOS method. Both the BMA and EMOS methods offer higher resolutions than the raw ensemble output across all lead times. Overall, the BMA method proves to be the most reliable method for 24-hour, 48-hour, and 72-hour forecasts, as compared to the EMOS-CSG and EMOS-GEV methods and the raw ensemble forecast, when evaluated over the studied region in local method. but in semi-local,all methods included  BMA, EMOS-CSG and EMOS-GEV have good results compared to raw and have high reliability."

In summary, the reliability of light and moderate precipitation levels for 24-hour accumulated rainfall is noteworthy. Moreover, at threshold values of 15 and 25 mm, the Bayesian Model Averaging (BMA) method demonstrates better calibration than the Ensemble Model Output Statistics (EMOS) method. Both BMA and EMOS methods exhibit higher resolutions than the raw ensemble output across all lead times in the local method. However, in the semi-local method, all methods, including BMA, EMOS-CSG, and EMOS-GEV, furnish reliable forecasts compared to the raw ensemble and offer high reliability. Overall, the BMA method is the most dependable technique for 24-hour, 48-hour, and 72-hour forecasts when assessed over the studied region in the local method.

Edit the Text. All references in the text should be in their place. "ROC diagrams for the probabilistic forecasts of 24-h cumulative precipitation using the raw ensemble forecast, and the BMA, and EMOS-CSG methods for local and semi-local for the 24-h, 48-h, and 72-h lead times."

The Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) diagrams illustrate the probabilistic forecasts of 24-hour accumulated precipitation using the raw ensemble forecast alongside the Bayesian Model Averaging (BMA) and Ensemble Model Output Statistics with climatology, persistence, and analogue predictor selection (EMOS-CSG) methods for both local and semi-local approaches across 24-hour, 48-hour, and 72-hour lead times.

Edit the Text. All references in the text should be in their place. "The ROC diagrams of the calibrated forecasts using the BMA, EMOS-CSG methods, and the raw ensemble forecast for the 24-h, 48-h, and 72-h lead times for local and semi-local in different thresholds are shown in Fig. 8. "

Figure 8 displays the Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) diagrams for calibrated forecasts using the BMA and EMOS-CSG methods, as well as the raw ensemble forecast, for lead times of 24, 48, and 72 hours, both in local and semi-local approaches and across various thresholds.
